title_en: Donald Trump has no good options for reopening the Strait of Hormuz
title_zh: 唐纳德·特朗普没有重新开放霍尔木兹海峡的好办法
rubric_en: Yet the stand-off has costs for cash-strapped Iran, too
rubric_zh: 然而，这场僵局对资金拮据的伊朗也有代价

paragraphs.en: When donald trump signed a preliminary peace deal with Iran last month, he bragged that it “achieves everything we set out to accomplish”. The Gulf war would end and the Strait of Hormuz would reopen. Less than a month later, the agreement looks to be unravelling: fighting has resumed and traffic through the strait has collapsed. This is occurring not in spite of the misnamed memorandum of understanding (mou) but because of it.
paragraphs.zh: 上个月，唐纳德·特朗普与伊朗签署一项初步和平协议时夸口说，它“实现了我们着手要完成的一切”。海湾战争将会结束，霍尔木兹海峡也将重新开放。不到一个月后，这项协议看起来正在瓦解：战斗已经恢复，通过海峡的航运量也已暴跌。出现这种局面，并不是尽管有这份名不副实的谅解备忘录（mou），而是因为它。

paragraphs.en: Overnight on July 15th Iran targeted American military installations in Bahrain and Kuwait as America continued its bombardment of targets in southern Iran and along the strait. There have been seven such nights of tit-for-tat strikes since July 7th, when Iran started the fighting by attacking three ships in the strait.
paragraphs.zh: 7月15日夜间，在美国继续轰炸伊朗南部以及海峡沿岸目标之际，伊朗瞄准了美国在巴林和科威特的军事设施。自7月7日以来，双方已经有七个夜晚进行了这种针锋相对的打击；当天伊朗袭击了海峡中的三艘船只，从而挑起战斗。

paragraphs.en: For now, both sides are limiting their attacks. America is mostly striking southern Iran. In turn, Iran has not attacked civilian infrastructure in the Gulf. Neither looks eager to return to all-out war.
paragraphs.zh: 眼下，双方都在限制攻击范围。美国主要打击伊朗南部。相应地，伊朗没有袭击海湾地区的民用基础设施。双方似乎都不急于重返全面战争。

paragraphs.en: Still, much of the mou is now a dead letter. America has revoked a sanctions waiver that allowed Iran to sell oil. On July 13th Mr Trump said he would also resume the blockade of Iranian shipping. On July 14th he repeated threats to target Iran’s bridges and power stations unless it returned to talks. The American president wants the strait back open—yet he has no good way to do it.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，谅解备忘录的大部分内容如今已成一纸空文。美国撤销了一项允许伊朗出售石油的制裁豁免。7月13日，特朗普表示他还将恢复对伊朗航运的封锁。7月14日，他再次威胁称，除非伊朗重返谈判，否则将把伊朗的桥梁和发电站作为目标。这位美国总统希望海峡重新开放，但他没有好办法做到这一点。

paragraphs.en: At the centre of the dispute is a disagreement over the mou. Its fifth paragraph states that Iran will “make arrangements…for the safe passage of commercial vessels”. Like much of the deal, the clause is vaguely worded. America reads it to mean that Iran will remove its mines and unlock the strait. Iran argues that it also confers a right to administer traffic.
paragraphs.zh: 争端的核心在于双方对谅解备忘录的理解不同。备忘录第五段规定，伊朗将“作出安排……确保商船安全通行”。和这项协议的许多内容一样，这一条款措辞含糊。美国将其理解为伊朗将清除水雷并解除对海峡的封锁。伊朗则主张，它还赋予伊朗管理通航的权利。

paragraphs.en: The usual route through the strait, known as the Traffic Separation Scheme, is a six-mile-wide corridor through its deepest waters. Mines there imperil ships, forcing vessels to choose between alternative passages: north through Iranian waters, or south through Oman’s. For weeks America’s navy has helped escort tankers through the second route. The un has also encouraged shippers to use it.
paragraphs.zh: 穿越海峡的通常航线被称为“分道通航制”，是一条穿过最深水域、宽六英里的走廊。那里的水雷威胁船只安全，迫使船舶在其他通道中选择：要么向北穿过伊朗水域，要么向南穿过阿曼水域。数周以来，美国海军一直协助护送油轮走第二条路线。联合国也鼓励航运公司使用这条航线。

paragraphs.en: The Iranians see this as a threat to their control of the strait and now say the Oman route is closed. America insists it remains open. Rhetoric matters less than reality: if shipowners think they might be attacked, most will consider Hormuz shut. Tanker traffic is at its lowest level since May 25th, says Kpler, a ship-tracker. Just 11 vessels transited the strait on July 12th, down from 36 a week earlier, according to Windward, another such firm. At first, oil markets shrugged off the fighting. Now they look more concerned. The price of Brent crude climbed to $87 a barrel on July 14th, before falling back to $84.50 at the time of publication, up by 17.2% from July 6th.
paragraphs.zh: 伊朗人认为这是对其控制海峡的威胁，如今声称阿曼路线已经关闭。美国坚称它仍然开放。言辞不如现实重要：如果船东认为自己可能遭到袭击，大多数人都会认为霍尔木兹海峡已经关闭。船舶追踪公司Kpler称，油轮通行量已降至5月25日以来的最低水平。另一家同类公司Windward称，7月12日仅有11艘船通过该海峡，而一周前为36艘。起初，石油市场对战事并不在意。如今，它们看起来更加担忧。7月14日，布伦特原油价格升至每桶87美元，随后在本文发表时回落至84.50美元，较7月6日上涨17.2%。

paragraphs.en: When the mou was signed, Mr Trump hoped that the promise of sanctions relief would persuade Iran to reopen the strait. The regime is desperate for cash. It claims the war caused $270bn in damage to an economy already reeling from years of sanctions and mismanagement. It signed the deal chiefly to secure financial benefits. Yet it now seems torn between relative pragmatists, who understand the depth of the economic crisis, and ultra-hardliners determined to keep Hormuz in their grasp.
paragraphs.zh: 谅解备忘录签署时，特朗普希望解除制裁的承诺能说服伊朗重新开放海峡。伊朗政权极度缺钱。它声称，这场战争给一个本已因多年制裁和治理不善而摇摇欲坠的经济造成了2700亿美元损失。它签署协议主要是为了获得经济利益。然而如今，它似乎在相对务实派和极端强硬派之间左右为难：前者明白经济危机有多深，后者则决心把霍尔木兹牢牢抓在手中。

paragraphs.en: America has thus turned back to pressure. One option for Mr Trump is to carry on with limited attacks on Iranian military sites and equipment. The trouble is that more than 300 such strikes over seven nights have not changed Iranian behaviour. There is no reason to think that an eighth night, or an eightieth, would make the difference. After six weeks of far more intense American and Israeli bombing, the benefits of occasional strikes today are probably marginal. Even if America could knock out all of Iran’s coastal launchers, the regime could continue firing at ships from inland. Many of its missiles and drones have ranges above 1,000km.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，美国又转回施压。特朗普的一个选择是继续对伊朗军事基地和装备进行有限攻击。问题在于，七个夜晚超过300次此类打击并未改变伊朗的行为。没有理由认为第八个夜晚，或第八十个夜晚，会带来不同结果。在美国和以色列进行了六周强度高得多的轰炸之后，如今偶尔打击的收益很可能只是边际性的。即便美国能够摧毁伊朗所有沿海发射装置，该政权仍可从内陆继续向船只开火。它的许多导弹和无人机射程超过1000公里。

paragraphs.en: A second option is to escalate. Yet doing so carries risk, and no guarantee of a return. Bombing vital infrastructure in Iran would invite reciprocal attacks on Gulf states (and might be a war crime). Deploying ground troops is almost certainly a non-starter in Washington. On July 8th Mr Trump repeated his frequent threat to “take over” Kharg island, home to Iran’s main oil-export terminal. This would not help reopen Hormuz, over 600km away.
paragraphs.zh: 第二个选择是升级冲突。然而这样做有风险，而且不能保证有回报。轰炸伊朗关键基础设施会招致对海湾国家的对等攻击（也可能构成战争罪）。在华盛顿，部署地面部队几乎肯定行不通。7月8日，特朗普再次重复他经常发出的威胁，要“接管”哈尔克岛；那里是伊朗主要石油出口终端所在地。这无助于重新开放600多公里外的霍尔木兹海峡。

paragraphs.en: Reinstating the blockade may seem less fraught. It brings America and Iran back to the situation that prevailed between early April, when they first announced a ceasefire, and the signing of the mou on June 17th. That was in effect a staring contest, to see which side blinked first under economic pressure. But the outcome was the interim deal that already looks to be falling apart. Nor is there a guarantee that Iran will accept the blockade a second time. If it were to resume bigger attacks on Gulf states, Mr Trump would have to choose between all-out war and retreat.
paragraphs.zh: 恢复封锁看起来也许没那么棘手。它使美国和伊朗回到4月初双方首次宣布停火到6月17日签署谅解备忘录之间的状态。那实际上是一场对峙，看哪一方会在经济压力下先眨眼。但结果正是那项如今已经显得摇摇欲坠的临时协议。也不能保证伊朗会第二次接受封锁。如果它恢复对海湾国家发动更大规模的攻击，特朗普就不得不在全面战争和退却之间作出选择。

paragraphs.en: His third option is to look elsewhere for help. Meaningful support will not be forthcoming. A multilateral mission to secure the strait exists on paper, and Britain and France are willing to work with Oman to clear mines. But renewed fighting will deter would-be participants from sending warships. Germany’s Bundestag failed to vote on the issue before its summer break. Japan remains undecided. Gulf states are reluctant to get involved (and their navies are probably not up to scratch), lest Iran retaliate. China has urged Iran to reopen the strait, but urging is all it has done.
paragraphs.zh: 他的第三个选择是到别处寻求帮助。但有实质意义的支持不会到来。纸面上存在一项确保海峡安全的多边任务，英国和法国也愿意与阿曼合作清除水雷。但战事重燃会让潜在参与方不愿派出军舰。德国联邦议院在夏季休会前未能就此事投票。日本仍未作出决定。海湾国家不愿卷入其中（而且它们的海军可能也不够过硬），以免伊朗报复。中国敦促伊朗重新开放海峡，但它所做的也仅止于敦促。

paragraphs.en: Mr Trump finds himself back where he started three months ago: neither carrots nor sticks can persuade Iran to reopen the strait. Yet the stand-off has real costs for Iran, too. Some hawks in Tehran argue that relinquishing their grip on Hormuz would deprive them of leverage in a future conflict. That misreads reality. So long as Iran has missiles and drones, it can shut the strait at will. Control of Hormuz matters to Iran today as a means, not an end: it is only valuable in a trade for tangible benefits.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普发现自己又回到了三个月前的起点：无论胡萝卜还是大棒，都无法说服伊朗重新开放海峡。然而，这场僵局对伊朗也有真实代价。德黑兰的一些鹰派认为，放弃对霍尔木兹的掌控会让他们在未来冲突中失去筹码。这误读了现实。只要伊朗拥有导弹和无人机，它就可以随时关闭海峡。今天，控制霍尔木兹对伊朗而言是一种手段，而不是目的：只有在用它交换实际利益时，它才有价值。

paragraphs.en: With sanctions restored and the blockade resuming, Iran has already lost its immediate gains from the mou. It cannot charge tolls on vessels using the strait in peacetime unless Oman agrees to collaborate. Meanwhile, much of the crude it exported during the first weeks of the deal is sitting unsold: Chinese refiners are instead buying from Arab states, which have priced their oil at a discount to Iran’s.
paragraphs.zh: 随着制裁恢复、封锁重启，伊朗已经失去了从谅解备忘录中获得的即时收益。除非阿曼同意合作，否则它无法在和平时期向使用海峡的船只收费。与此同时，它在协议最初几周出口的许多原油仍滞销未售：中国炼油商转而从阿拉伯国家购买原油，因为这些国家的油价相对于伊朗原油有所折扣。

paragraphs.en: Neither side has a military route to achieving its goals. Mr Trump cannot bomb the strait open; Iran cannot profit if it is shut. The least bad option for both is the deal they are busy shooting to bits. ■
paragraphs.zh: 双方都没有通过军事手段实现目标的途径。特朗普无法靠轰炸把海峡炸开；伊朗也无法在海峡关闭时获利。对双方来说，最不坏的选择正是他们正忙着打得千疮百孔的那项协议。■

paragraphs.en: Sign up to the Middle East Dispatch, a weekly newsletter that keeps you in the loop on a fascinating, complex and consequential part of the world.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅《中东快讯》，这是一份每周一期的通讯，帮助你及时了解世界上这个迷人、复杂且影响重大的地区。
